cases.description: Loads the full host adapter baseline from spec/hosts.json.
Resolves legacy or ecosystem-compatible host aliases to canonical adapter ids.
Resolves the kimi-code-cli alias to the canonical kimi-cli adapter id.
Reports a structured error when an explicit host id or alias is unknown.
Maps missing install flags to the user default while recording that scope was not explicit.
Maps repeated and comma-separated agent flags to a deduplicated explicit selector.
Maps --agent '*' to the all-host selector.
Reports invalid scope and mixed star agent selector flags.
Copies once when many supported agents share the .agents/skills project target.
Installs a bundled skill into the canonical user scope for an explicit host.
Installs Codex user-scope skills into the first canonical user path when multiple user paths are valid.
Installs a bundled skill into the canonical project scope for an explicit host.
Reports the project-scope install target without writing skill files.
Installs into a project-only host when project scope is selected explicitly.
Reports an unsupported-scope error when a project-only host is selected for user scope.
Installs only into the explicitly selected host targets.
Uses detection paths to select installed hosts for automatic installation.
Returns an empty report when automatic host detection finds no supported hosts.
Copies once when multiple selected hosts resolve to the same canonical target directory.
Skips a kitup-owned target when its metadata hash matches the bundled skill.
Does not render a user-facing summary or confirmation when every selected target is unchanged.
Returns a conflict exit classification when the plan has conflicts but no writes.

# Embedded

cases.description: Explicit agents select canonical hosts without detection.
Agent star explicitly selects every supported host.
TTY workflow prompts for scope before planning an explicit agent install when scope was not provided.
Non-TTY workflow refuses to choose scope implicitly when scope was not provided.
Yes mode uses the configured default scope when scope was not provided.
Shared target install still renders one user-facing row per selected host.
TTY with no detected hosts asks the user to choose from supported hosts.
TTY with one detected host can select it before summary confirmation.
TTY with multiple detected hosts enters agent multi-selection instead of auto-installing all.
TTY multi-select with an empty selection cancels instead of installing every detected host.
TTY multi-select installs only the selected host after summary confirmation.
TTY with one detected host still renders a summary and waits for confirmation before writing.
Yes mode skips prompts and installs every detected host explicitly selected by policy.
Yes mode can accept detected hosts non-interactively.
Non-TTY without explicit agent or yes cannot enter interactive selection.
Yes mode does not turn zero detection into all-host installation.
Installs a public GitHub bundle with provenance metadata while keeping the user-facing install flow unchanged.
